varieties.name: European Goldenrod (Solidago virgaurea) - HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, Asia, North Africa
varieties.traits: Upright perennial, 20-100cm tall, cylindrical stems, pointed elliptical leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate; less invasive than North American species
varieties.growing_notes: BEST FOR humid subtropical climates - Non-invasive, better heat tolerance than N. American species, medicinal herb with traditional wound-healing uses. Less aggressive spreading.
varieties.availability: Australian herb nurseries (Mudbrick Herb Cottage, All Rare Herbs, The Seed Collection)

varieties.name: 'Peter Pan' European Goldenrod (S. virgaurea 'Peter Pan') - HYBRID
varieties.yield_notes: Good flower production for compact size
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT humid subtropical climates CHOICE - Heat tolerant, afternoon shade helpful in warm climates, compact size ideal for gardens, disease-resistant

varieties.name: Alpine Goldenrod (S. virgaurea var. alpina/minuta) - HEIRLOOM
varieties.growing_notes: Compact dwarf form suitable for rock gardens, tolerates drought and poor soils, good humid subtropical climates option for contained spaces

varieties.name: 'Baby Sun' Goldenrod (Solidago hybrid) - HYBRID
varieties.traits: Compact 60cm height, upright habit, early blooming
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for humid subtropical climates with good drainage, compact for small gardens, top-rated in Chicago Botanic trials, non-aggressive

varieties.name: 'Goldkind' (Golden Baby) Goldenrod - HYBRID
varieties.growing_notes: Disease-resistant important for humid humid subtropical summers, comparable size to Baby Sun, well-rated for garden cultivation

varieties.name: 'Fireworks' Goldenrod (S. rugosa 'Fireworks') - HYBRID/OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: North Carolina Botanical Garden selection (1993), from coastal plain population
varieties.growing_notes: CAUTION FOR humid subtropical climates - Prefers slightly acidic soils, tolerates wet conditions better than others, but may struggle with extreme humid subtropical climates heat. Plant in part shade. Top-rated in temperate trials.

varieties.name: 'Golden Fleece' Goldenrod (S. sphacelata 'Golden Fleece') - HYBRID
varieties.origin: Mt. Cuba Center selection (1985), from Eden, North Carolina seedling
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf 30-60cm, heart-shaped toothed foliage, slow rhizome spread
varieties.growing_notes: GOOD humid subtropical climates OPTION - Compact size, tolerates drought and poor soils, won international awards (ISU Award). Thrives in sunny well-drained sites.

varieties.name: Seaside Goldenrod (S. sempervirens) - OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: Native to eastern North America coastal areas, Newfoundland to Central America
varieties.traits: Succulent evergreen perennial, 120-180cm tall, thick hairless leaves, salt-tolerant
varieties.growing_notes: POTENTIAL humid subtropical climates COASTAL CHOICE - Extremely salt, drought, and humidity tolerant. Succulent foliage adapted to coastal conditions. May suit humid subtropical climates coastal gardens. Prefers sandy soils.
varieties.availability: Import required; not commonly available in Australia

varieties.name: Sweet Goldenrod (S. odora) - HEIRLOOM/OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: Native eastern North America, New Hampshire to Texas and Mexico
varieties.traits: Slender upright 60-120cm, anise/licorice-scented leaves, lance-shaped foliage with translucent dots
varieties.growing_notes: Moderate humid subtropical climates potential - Non-aggressive clump former, drought-tolerant, historically used for Blue Mountain Tea (medicinal). Anise-scented foliage distinctive. Prefers dry sandy soils.

varieties.name: White Goldenrod/Silverrod (S. bicolor) - OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.origin: Native eastern North America (Manitoba to Nova Scotia, south to Georgia)
varieties.traits: Narrow upright 30-90cm, thin wiry stems, gray-downy foliage
varieties.growing_notes: UNIQUE COLOR - Only white-flowered Solidago in eastern North America. Tolerates clay soils, drought, poor soils. May suit humid subtropical climates if drainage good.

varieties.name: Stiff Goldenrod (S. rigida) - OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.traits: Tall 150+ cm, rigid upright stems, gray-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: CAUTION - Very tall, needs space. Tolerates heat and drought well. May work in humid subtropical climates with afternoon shade and ample space.

varieties.name: European Goldenrod (S. virgaurea) - EXPERIMENTAL HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, Asia (incl. parts of Turkey, Kazakhstan, northern India Himalayas)
varieties.growing_notes: While Asian subspecies exist (S. virgaurea subsp. asiatica in temperate regions), none are adapted to Tropical monsoon tropical monsoon. Would require artificial cooling, fungicide program. NOT RECOMMENDED.
varieties.availability: Not available in tropical monsoon climates; import would be required and likely unsuccessful

varieties.name: Seaside Goldenrod (S. sempervirens) - EXPERIMENTAL OPEN-POLLINATED
varieties.growing_notes: While succulent leaves and salt tolerance seem promising, plant still requires seasonal temperature variation. Monsoon rains combined with heat would likely cause root rot and foliar diseases.

varieties.name: Sweet Goldenrod (S. odora) - EXPERIMENTAL HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Eastern USA, south to Mexico (but in drier regions)
varieties.growing_notes: Absolutely unsuitable for tropical monsoon climates monsoon conditions. Anise-scented tea use doesn't compensate for complete climatic mismatch.
